headings: The book is installed, not read
You need only two things
Two lines open the box
A fresh session proves it worked
Three checks find what failed
Never copy it
Words come before promises
You arrive at the words together
This book's pictures belong to one owner
Only six things need names now
Each kitchen keeps its own words
Shared language can fail quietly
Never make one kitchen's language the law
Chapter 1 chose the words and this chapter fixes their meanings

Your work word names the people who make what was asked for

Your unit-of-work word names the thing that moves from maker to checker
Never send the owner a task to perform
This chapter gives the building its first definition
The building stays when you move premises
Familiar parts of the entrance show the boundary
Two answers settle every doubtful case
This chapter says what exists, not what happens there
One test keeps the category steady
Furniture can leave without becoming less

Equipment is powered and does work for you
One test keeps the category steady
Powered things stay separate from the things around them
The cooks' appliance belongs here too
This chapter records what exists

The category exists before any directions begin

Each ingredient comes with who supplies it

Never call every replaceable thing stock
Money is anything that costs or earns
The exact thing must pass the test

The owner's daily amount is a decision already made
Money exists before anyone handles it
A role survives a change of staff

A role must be stated, never assumed
Seven jobs require three separate pairs of hands
Three checks answer three different questions
Sickness removes service before it removes a check

The manual is the one thing that holds still

Every instruction travels unchanged

Never let one restaurant fix its own recipe

A changed answer replaces the old answer

Two kinds of record must never become one thing
The log answers what we did
Every order receives seven stamps
Open orders become the kitchen’s job list

Four reasons make the log necessary
Never improve the past
Protection does not wait to be remembered

The doorman keeps cooks at separate benches

This chapter furnishes protection without running it

Count the roles rather than the names

Never promise to check later

Never rewrite an order while handing it over

Never leave prep in the cook's memory

Never improve one dish locally
The dish leaves the maker's hands before judgment

The two verdicts reveal four outcomes
Send it means leave it ready on the pass counter

Never turn obedience into quality

Never turn carrying into approval

Never let washing reach back into the order
Closing comes after clearing and before reading
The head chef closes both records

Never make one tidy closing sheet

The diary shows the day that happened
Different waste belongs to different eyes

The log remembers after every shift leaves
Never improve the diary while reading it
Every employee can open the door from below

Nobody in the restaurant repairs the manual
Never turn a fault into a private improvement
The doctor asks whether the means are still fit

Never let the doctor treat the fault

Never rewrite the past after a repair

Never turn handover into closing and opening

Instructions travel and records never do

Never rewrite the promise on arrival

The restaurant itself starts blank

Never accept a private improvement

Never turn the menu into a recipe
